project: Model Atlas — Phase 1 Entry and Index Routes
description: Close the Phase 1 discovery route gap by shipping a docs-native Model Atlas home, documented search entry at /search, architecture and tags index routes, the attention tag landing page, shell navigation, and route smoke tests—using existing registry and content loaders and linking to grouped-query attention and token without Phase 2 content expansion.

context.customerAsk: Phase 1 completion: implement the missing discovery and index routes without broad Phase 2 content expansion. The app must render a docs-native Model Atlas home/reference entry instead of a placeholder; a search entry route at /search or an equivalent documented route; an architecture index route; a tags index route; and an attention tag landing page at /tags/attention. The routes should use the existing registry, tag, module, and glossary loaders, link to grouped-query attention and token, avoid raw placeholder copy, and preserve the dark technical atlas visual direction from docs/site-fundamentals.md. Acceptance: next build lists the new static routes, navigation exposes them, and route smoke tests cover home, architecture, glossary, tags, attention tag, token, and grouped-query attention.
context.problem: Readers still see placeholder home copy and cannot reach search entry, architecture index, tags index, or /tags/attention, blocking Phase 1 manual review and factory checklist completion for discovery routes.
context.solution: Add registry-backed browse helpers and thin App Router pages for home, /search, architecture index, /tags, and /tags/attention; extend DocsShell navigation and localized UI messages; align glossary index with the shell; and add route smoke tests—deferring full Orama search UI to the sibling search-experience work item.

acceptanceCriteria: / renders a docs-native Model Atlas reference home with links to search, architecture, glossary, tags, grouped-query attention, and token—no placeholder copy.
/search renders a documented search entry page with localized copy and a visible search handoff affordance.
Architecture index lists published architecture-related entries with title, summary, and links, plus an accessible empty state when none match.
/tags lists published tag records with category labels and links to tag landing pages.
/tags/attention lists attention-tagged resources grouped by kind, including grouped-query attention when published.
Primary navigation exposes Home, Search, Architecture, Glossary, and Tags on discovery routes.
next build succeeds and emits static routes for the new discovery paths.
Route smoke tests cover home, architecture index, glossary, tags, attention tag, token, grouped-query attention, and search entry.
Typecheck, lint, and tests pass via make ci.

userStories.title: Add browse helpers for architecture entries and tag resources
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need shared loaders that list architecture-related registry entries and resolve tag membership so index routes stay thin and testable.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/content exposes a function to list published architecture-related browse entries with resolved title, summary, and canonical URL.
src/lib/content exposes a function to list published tag records with slug, localized title or summary, and category.
src/lib/content exposes a function to list published resources for a tag slug grouped by record kind, omitting empty groups.
For tag attention, grouped-query attention appears under modules when registry or frontmatter includes attention.
Unit tests cover attention tag membership and at least one architecture browse entry.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Extend UI messages and primary navigation
userStories.description: As a reader, I want consistent navigation labels and links so I can reach discovery routes from any shell-wrapped page.
acceptanceCriteria: src/content/messages/en/common.json defines message keys for nav labels and discovery page titles.
DocsShell primary navigation includes links to /, /search, architecture index, /docs/glossary, and /tags with focus rings using the ring token.
Navigation removes placeholder-only targets such as Open docs placeholder.
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: header shows Home, Search, Architecture, Glossary, and Tags on the home route.

userStories.title: Render Model Atlas home reference entry
userStories.description: As a reader, I want the site root to explain Model Atlas and point me to search, indexes, and the Phase 1 sample pages.
acceptanceCriteria: / uses DocsShell with dark atlas tokens per docs/site-fundamentals.md.
Home content resolves from localized message keys without raw placeholder prose in the route component.
Home includes brush-stroke header treatment and article-flow sections, not a dashboard grid.
Home links to /search, architecture index, /docs/glossary, /tags, /docs/modules/grouped-query-attention, and /docs/glossary/token.
No placeholder copy appears in the rendered home body or primary CTA.
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: / shows Model Atlas branding, sample links, and dark documentation styling.

userStories.title: Add documented search entry route
userStories.description: As a reader, I want a dedicated search entry URL so bookmarks and tag handoffs can target search consistently.
acceptanceCriteria: /search renders inside the docs shell with localized title and description from message keys.
The canonical search entry path is documented if an equivalent route is used instead of /search.
The page provides a visible search handoff control suitable for wiring to global search later; it must not 404 or show lorem placeholder text.
Tag landing pages can link to /search with an optional tag query parameter per the data-model handoff pattern.
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: /search loads with accessible heading and handoff affordance.

userStories.title: Render architecture index route
userStories.description: As a reader, I want one page listing architecture-related reference material so I can browse structural concepts before opening individual docs.
acceptanceCriteria: Architecture index route exists at a single documented path used consistently in navigation.
The page lists browse entries with title, summary, and link sorted alphabetically by title.
Token glossary or token concept appears with a correct link when it qualifies as architecture-related.
When no entries qualify, the page shows an accessible empty state with guidance to home or search.
User-visible strings use message keys and semantic shadcn tokens.
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: architecture index lists the Phase 1 token entry when published.

userStories.title: Render tags index route
userStories.description: As a reader, I want to browse all controlled tags and open tag landing pages without knowing slugs in advance.
acceptanceCriteria: /tags lists published tag records with localized title or summary, category label, and link to /tags/<slug>.
Tags sort or group by category from the tag schema; tag chips remain monochrome by default.
Published tags attention and kv-cache appear with working links when present in registry.
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: /tags shows attention and kv-cache entries linking to their landing pages.

userStories.title: Render attention tag landing page
userStories.description: As a reader exploring attention mechanisms, I want a tag page listing every published resource tagged attention.
acceptanceCriteria: /tags/attention resolves the attention tag record and renders resources grouped by kind via TagResourceList or equivalent.
Grouped-query attention appears under modules with link to /docs/modules/grouped-query-attention.
Token glossary or token concept appears when tagged attention in registry or frontmatter.
Page includes a search handoff link to /search and uses message keys for headings and empty copy.
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: /tags/attention lists GQA and related attention-tagged resources.

userStories.title: Align glossary index with shell and message keys
userStories.description: As a reader, I want the glossary index to match other discovery pages in navigation, localization, and empty-state behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: /docs/glossary uses the same docs shell and navigation as other discovery routes.
Glossary index copy resolves from message keys; token entry remains listed with title, summary, and link.
Empty state matches the accessible pattern used on architecture and tags indexes.
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: glossary index is reachable from primary nav and lists Token.

userStories.title: Add route smoke tests and build route verification
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want automated checks that Phase 1 discovery routes exist so regressions are caught before manual review.
acceptanceCriteria: Bun tests smoke-check that App Router page modules exist for /, /search, architecture index, /docs/glossary, /tags, /tags/attention, /docs/glossary/token, and /docs/modules/grouped-query-attention.
Tests assert browse helpers return attention-tagged resources including grouped-query attention in the Phase 1 baseline.
next build succeeds; new static routes are present in build output.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
